# Demo

A short audio sample (`opening.mp3`, ~30 s) of the famous opening passage of
*Pride and Prejudice* by Jane Austen, generated by this pipeline. Public
domain, via [Project Gutenberg](https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/1342).

## Regenerating the demo

1. Download the EPUB from Project Gutenberg:
   <https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/1342>
   (use the EPUB download, not the HTML).

2. Save it locally — for example, `pg1342.epub` in this folder. **The EPUB
   itself is gitignored** — the binary doesn't get committed.

3. Build a cast file:

   ```bash
   python cast_builder.py --epub demos/pg1342.epub --book-id pride_prejudice
   ```

4. Edit `cast_pride_prejudice.json` to set voices for the major characters
   (Elizabeth → eve, Darcy → rex, Bingley → leo, Jane → ara, etc.). See
   [`../examples/cast.example.json`](../examples/cast.example.json) for the
   schema.

5. Render the first chapter (the opening passage is at the very start):

   ```bash
   python render_batch.py \
     --epub demos/pg1342.epub \
     --book-id pride_prejudice \
     --chapters 1 \
     --multi-voice \
     --cast cast_pride_prejudice.json
   ```

6. Trim the first ~30 seconds for the demo with ffmpeg:

   ```bash
   ffmpeg -i library/pride_prejudice/chapter_001.mp3 -t 30 -c copy demos/opening.mp3
   ```

The result is what gets committed as `demos/opening.mp3` so README readers
can hear the system without running anything.
